req.title: External ACH transfer
req.statement: A customer shall transfer funds to an external ACH-linked account up to $10,000 per transfer; transfers above this shall require step-up verification.

doc.reasoning: The requirement defines three distinct equivalence classes: (1) transfers <= $10,000 with verified ACH link — should succeed; (2) transfers > $10,000 without step-up — should return 422; (3) transfers with insufficient funds — should return 409. Boundary values are $10,000 (exact limit, valid) and $10,001 (just above limit, invalid without step-up). A state transition exists from 'pending' to 'queued' on success. Decision table covers the cross-product of amount threshold × step-up presence × fund availability.

tests.name: test_ach_transfer_valid_amount_within_limit
preconditions: Customer account exists and is authenticated
Transfer amount $5,000 is within the $10,000 limit
actions: POST /transfers/ach with amount=5000 and valid ACH account reference
Capture HTTP response status and body
trace: R-FIN-004: transfers up to $10,000 shall be accepted
R-FIN-004: ACH batch queued on success
tests.executable:
def test_ach_transfer_valid_amount_within_limit(ach_client, verified_ach_account, funded_customer_token):
    payload = {
        "amount": 5000,
        "currency": "USD",
        "destination_account_id": verified_ach_account["id"]
    }
    resp = ach_client.post(
        "/transfers/ach",
        json=payload,
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {funded_customer_token}"}
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 202
    body = resp.json()
    assert "transfer_id" in body
    assert body["status"] == "queued"
    confirmation = <fetch_confirmation_event>(body["transfer_id"])
    assert confirmation is not None

tests.name: test_ach_transfer_above_limit_without_step_up_returns_422
preconditions: Customer account exists and is authenticated
actions: POST /transfers/ach with amount=15000 and no step-up header
Capture HTTP response status and body
trace: R-FIN-004: transfers above $10,000 require step-up verification
tests.executable:
def test_ach_transfer_above_limit_without_step_up_returns_422(ach_client, verified_ach_account, funded_customer_token):
    payload = {
        "amount": 15000,
        "currency": "USD",
        "destination_account_id": verified_ach_account["id"]
    }
    resp = ach_client.post(
        "/transfers/ach",
        json=payload,
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {funded_customer_token}"}
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 422
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("error_code") == "STEP_UP_REQUIRED"
    assert "transfer_id" not in body
    queued = <check_ach_batch_queued>(funded_customer_token, amount=15000)
    assert queued is False

tests.name: test_ach_transfer_at_exact_limit_boundary_is_accepted
preconditions: Customer account exists and is authenticated
Transfer amount is exactly $10,000 — the upper boundary
actions: POST /transfers/ach with amount=10000 and no step-up header
Capture HTTP response status and body
trace: R-FIN-004: transfers up to $10,000 shall be accepted (boundary inclusive)
R-FIN-004: ACH batch queued on success

tests.name: test_ach_transfer_one_cent_above_limit_without_step_up_returns_422
preconditions: Customer account exists and is authenticated
Transfer amount $10,000.01 is one cent above the boundary
actions: POST /transfers/ach with amount=10000.01 and no step-up header
Capture HTTP response status and body
trace: R-FIN-004: transfers above $10,000 require step-up (off-by-one boundary)

tests.name: test_ach_transfer_insufficient_funds_returns_409
preconditions: Customer account exists and is authenticated
Account balance is $100 — insufficient for the requested $5,000 transfer
Transfer amount $5,000 is within the $10,000 limit
actions: POST /transfers/ach with amount=5000 for an underfunded account
Capture HTTP response status and body
trace: R-FIN-004: decision table row — valid amount + no step-up needed + insufficient balance

tests.name: test_ach_transfer_above_limit_with_step_up_transitions_to_queued
preconditions: Customer account exists and is authenticated
Customer has completed step-up verification — step-up token is valid
actions: Obtain step-up token via <get_step_up_token> for the customer
POST /transfers/ach with amount=15000 and X-Step-Up-Token header
Capture HTTP response status and body
trace: R-FIN-004: transfers above $10,000 accepted after step-up verification
R-FIN-004: ACH batch queued; confirmation sent
R-FIN-004: state transition pending -> queued covered
